---
title: "UIAO — Governance Substrate for Identity-First Modernization"
subtitle: "Canon-anchored · Drift-detected · FedRAMP-Moderate"
date: 2026-04-28
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
UIAO is under active development (v0.4.0, April 2026). The canon is authoritative, but
components are at varying stages of adoption. Check the [Substrate Status](docs/substrate-status.html)
page for per-component state. Pages describing capabilities that are canonically declared
but not yet fully adopted carry an **Aspirational** callout.
:::

## The problem

Every FedRAMP-Moderate environment drifts. Conditional Access policies get modified outside
change control. Directory objects accumulate without provenance. Telemetry pipelines go
silent and nobody notices until the next assessment. The gap between what your System
Security Plan says and what your tenant actually does is where audit findings live.

The compliance tools you already run — [CISA ScubaGear](https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGear),
tenant configuration exports, manual evidence binders — generate *signals*. But signals
without provenance are just noise. Nobody traces a policy change back to the person, the
approval, and the canon entry that authorized it — because no layer exists to do that.

## What UIAO is

UIAO is a **governance substrate** — not a product, not a platform, not a dashboard.
It is the architectural layer that sits above your compliance tools and below your
operational decisions, enforcing a single invariant:

> **Every governance claim traces to a certificate-anchored source of truth, or it doesn't exist.**

UIAO does not replace your compliance tools. It consumes their outputs — starting with
CISA ScubaGear — and provides the provenance, drift detection, and remediation
orchestration they were never designed to deliver.

| Capability | What it delivers |
|---|---|
| **Canon protection** | A protected corpus of authoritative schemas, registries, and policies. One source of truth per claim. No backfilling — every change carries a provenance chain. |
| **Drift detection** | Continuous monitoring across five drift classes: schema, semantic, provenance, authorization, and identity. Four severity tiers (P1–P4) with SLA-enforced remediation. |
| **Adapter architecture** | 13 adapters (conformance + modernization) that normalize heterogeneous tool outputs into a unified governance signal. Two operational classes × five mission classes. |
| **163 Key Security Indicators** | Cryptographically signed, continuously evaluated, machine-verifiable compliance signals — each traceable to its canonical source. |
| **SCuBA integration** | Consumes CISA ScubaGear JSON/CSV outputs. UIAO is the governance orchestration layer *above* ScubaGear — complementary, not competitive. |

## Who this is for

If you operate Microsoft 365 in a FedRAMP-Moderate boundary and your identity, telemetry,
or policy enforcement has ever drifted between what your SSP says and what your tenant
actually does — UIAO is built for exactly that problem.

- **Federal CISOs and CIOs** evaluating identity-driven zero trust architecture
- **FedRAMP Assessors (3PAOs)** reviewing continuous monitoring posture
- **Compliance Officers** responsible for SSP currency, POA&M management, and audit evidence
- **Security Architects** designing zero trust network access in hybrid cloud environments
- **IT Modernization Program Managers** replacing legacy ICAM, directory, and network infrastructure

## Get started

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### 10-minute quickstart {.unnumbered}

Run a full auditor bundle — evidence, POA&M, SSP narrative — against synthetic ScubaGear
fixtures. No Azure tenant, no API keys, no live data.

[Start the quickstart →](docs/quickstart.html)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Adapter authoring {.unnumbered}

Build a conformance or modernization adapter from scratch in 30 minutes, using the shipped
ScubaGear adapter as a worked example.

[Start the tutorial →](docs/adapter-authoring-tutorial.html)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Customer documents {.unnumbered}

Executive briefs, architecture specs, compliance mappings, modernization guides — organized
by pillar (Modernization, Compliance, Substrate).

[Browse documents →](customer-documents/index.html)
:::

::::

## Architecture at a glance

UIAO operates in **Commercial Cloud as governed by FedRAMP** — GCC-Moderate for Microsoft 365
SaaS services. It does not target GCC-High or DoD environments.

The substrate enforces four architectural invariants:

1. **Single Source of Truth (SSOT)** per governance claim, anchored in certificates and
   provenance chains that cannot be backfilled.
2. **Immutable canon** — authoritative artifacts are never deleted, only deprecated with a
   `superseded_by` pointer and full provenance.
3. **Machine-trackable provenance** — every derived artifact carries a provenance header
   tracing to its canonical source.
4. **CI-gated governance** — schema validation, metadata compliance, drift detection, and
   linting gate every PR into `main`.

## Navigate this site

| Section | What you'll find |
|---|---|
| [**Getting started**](docs/quickstart.html) | Quickstart, adapter tutorial, CLI reference |
| [**Findings**](findings/index.html) | Security findings registry and templates |
| [**Academy**](academy/index.html) | Training modules and conceptual walkthroughs |
| [**Modernization**](modernization/index.html) | Identity OrgTree, directory migration, codebook, dynamic groups |
| [**Customer Documents**](customer-documents/index.html) | Three-pillar document library: Modernization · Compliance · Substrate |

---

::: {.text-muted .text-center}
[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao) ·
[Changelog](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) ·
[Contributing](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ·
Licensed under [Apache 2.0](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/LICENSE)
:::
